entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A legume crop characterized by natural cross-pollination mediated by insects.

entities.name: NATURAL CROSS-POLLINATION
entities.description: A biological phenomenon where pollen is transferred between different plants of the same species, often mediated by insects.

entities.name: INSECTS
entities.description: Biological agents that mediate the process of natural cross-pollination in pigeonpea.

entities.name: OUT-CROSSING
entities.description: The transfer of pollen from one plant to another, contributing to genetic diversity.

entities.name: GENETIC PURITY
entities.description: The maintenance of a specific genetic makeup in cultivars and elite genetic stocks.

entities.name: CULTIVARS
entities.description: Plant varieties produced through selective breeding for specific desirable traits.

entities.name: VISUAL SELECTION
entities.description: A breeding technique of identifying natural hybrids based on observable physical traits in the field.

entities.name: NATURAL HYBRIDS
entities.description: Offspring resulting from the natural cross-pollination of different parent plants.

entities.name: LANDRACES
entities.description: Local varieties of crops that have evolved over time in specific environments.

entities.name: HIGH YIELD
entities.description: The capacity of a crop variety to produce a large amount of harvestable product.

entities.name: WILD RELATIVES OF PIGEONPEA
entities.description: Species closely related to the cultivated pigeonpea found in the wild.

entities.name: CYTOPLASMIC MALE STERILITY
entities.description: A genetic condition where plants cannot produce functional pollen, useful for hybrid seed production.

entities.name: HYBRID BREEDING TECHNOLOGY
entities.description: A systematic approach to developing hybrid varieties to improve crop performance.

entities.name: SEED PRODUCTION
entities.description: The process of producing high-quality seeds for commercial agricultural use.
